lede: A look at October 11 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Maja Chwalińska and Nathan Coulter-Nile, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Soyuz MS-10
events.desc: Soyuz MS-10, launching an intended crew for the ISS, suffers an in-flight abort. The crew lands safely.

events.title: Myyrmanni bombing
events.desc: A bomb attack in a Myyrmanni shopping mall in Vantaa, Finland kills seven.

events.title: Apollo 7
events.desc: NASA launches Apollo 7, the first successful crewed Apollo mission.

events.title: Field-sequential color system
events.desc: CBS's field-sequential color system for television is the first to be licensed for broadcast by the U.S. Federal Communications Commission.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Off Guadalcanal, United States Navy ships intercept and defeat a Japanese force.

events.title: Edward VIII
events.desc: The Duke and Duchess of Windsor tour Nazi Germany for 12 days and meet Adolf Hitler on 22 October.

events.title: Morant Bay rebellion
events.desc: Hundreds of black men and women march in Jamaica, starting the Morant Bay rebellion.

events.title: University of Sydney
events.desc: The University of Sydney, Australia's oldest university, is inaugurated in Sydney.

events.title: Bashir Shihab II
events.desc: The Maronite leader Bashir Shihab II surrenders to the Ottoman Empire and later is sent to Malta in exile.

events.title: Treaty of Shaoxing
events.desc: A peace treaty ends the Jin–Song wars.

births.name: David Morse
births.line: American actor, director, producer, and screenwriter

births.name: Amitabh Bachchan
births.line: Indian film actor, producer, television host, and former politician

deaths.text: Champ Summers, American baseball player and coach
Luc-Marie Bayle, French historian, photographer, and painter
MacKinlay Kantor, American journalist, author, and screenwriter

observances.text: Christian feast days: Andronicus, Probus, and Tarachus (Roman Catholic Church)
